title: 10 best herbal tinctures for sunburn
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for sunburn are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, typically made by soaking plant material in alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures help alleviate sunburn symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and regenerative properties. Some examples include calendula, St. John's wort, and lavender. Calendula tincture reduces redness and inflammation, promoting skin repair. St. John's wort has antiseptic qualities that help prevent infection and decrease pain. Lavender tincture soothes skin, eases irritation, and encourages tissue regeneration. By incorporating herbal tinctures into sunburn treatment, individuals can experience faster healing, reduced discomfort, and improved quality of life during recovery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture helps with sunburn because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds, such as bradykinase, that help to reduce pain and inflammation. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to increase collagen production, which can help to repair damaged skin cells and promote healing. The plant also contains antioxidants, such as vitamins C and E, that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can further damage sunburned skin. Furthermore, aloe vera has moisturizing properties that help to soothe and hydrate sunburned skin, preventing dryness and peeling. All of these properties make aloe vera tincture an effective natural remedy for treating sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tincture reduces inflammation and redness in sunburned skin, containing active constituents like bradykinase, salicylic acid, and prostaglandin inhibitors that help alleviate pain and swelling.
Moisturizing: Its ability to lock in moisture helps prevent dryness and peeling, thanks to the polysaccharides and sterols present in aloe vera that form a protective layer on the skin.
Wound healing: Aloe vera promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, containing compounds like acemannan, gibberellins, and lectins that stimulate cell growth and collagen synthesis.

Pain relief: The analgesic properties of aloe vera tincture help soothe and relieve sunburn pain, as it contains compounds like carboxypeptidase, salicylic acid, and bradykinase that inhibit pain signals.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera tincture neutralizes free radicals and reduces oxidative stress, thanks to the presence of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as various phenolic compounds, which protect skin cells from damage and support skin health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a clear gel that is often used to soothe sunburned skin. This gel can be steeped in hot water to make a medicinal tea.
Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a slimy substance that can be used to make a soothing tea for sunburn. This substance helps to hydrate and cool the skin.

Flowers: The flowers of the aloe vera plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by the sun. These antioxidants can be extracted and used to make a medicinal tea for sunburn.

Seeds: The seeds of the aloe vera plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. These fatty acids can be extracted and used to make a medicinal tea for sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves, cutting them close to the base; let them sit for 10 minutes to allow sap to drain.
Rinse the leaves under cool water, then fillet them to extract the clear gel, discarding the green skin.
Finely chop the gel and place it in a clean jar, filling it up to three-quarters; add 80-proof vodka until the gel is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two weeks, shaking it gently every other day.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid; store the tincture in a dark glass bottle and apply it to sunburned skin as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties that can soothe and repair damaged skin. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, pot marigold tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals. When applied topically, the tincture can help moisturize and rejuvenate the skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of sunburn. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a natural and effective remedy for sunburn that can provide quick relief and long-term benefits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for sunburn as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and pain. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing, crucial for sunburn as it helps repair damaged skin. This is attributed to the plant's triterpene content.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial properties, preventing infections in sunburned skin. This is due to the presence of thiophene derivatives.
Antioxidant: The tincture has antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals caused by sun exposure. This is thanks to the high levels of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Soothing: Pot marigold tincture has a soothing effect, providing relief from the discomfort of sunburn. This is due to the plant's saponins and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, making them useful for relieving pain and promoting skin regeneration after sunburn.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils with calming and soothing effects, which can help alleviate discomfort and redness caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh pot marigold flowers, rinse and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop flowers and place in a clean jar, filling 3/4 full, then add 80-proof vodka until flowers are fully submerged.
Seal jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out excess liquid.
Label bottle with ingredients, preparation date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply as needed to sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help soothe and reduce redness and inflammation caused by sunburn. The active compounds in chamomile, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, have been shown to help promote skin healing and regeneration, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, chamomile has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, making it an effective natural remedy for sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain, due to the presence of the active constituent, azulene.
Wound-healing: Its wound-healing properties promote skin regeneration and tissue repair, helping damaged skin recover from sunburn, thanks to the flavonoids and essential oils found in chamomile.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tincture contains antioxidants that protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation, reducing the oxidative stress that contributes to sunburn, attributed to the high levels of terpenoids and phenolic acids.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of German chamomile tincture help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with sunburn, as the active constituent, matricin, is converted to chamazulene, providing a soothing effect.
Moisturizing: German chamomile tincture helps keep the skin hydrated and moisturized, reducing dryness and peeling caused by sunburn, due to the essential oils and waxes present in the plant.
remedy_parts: Flowers: German chamomile flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the German chamomile plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to help reduce redness and irritation caused by sunburn.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a great source of essential oils, which have been found to have a calming effect on sunburned skin, helping to alleviate pain and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or muslin cloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent flowers.
Store the finished tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. Apply a few drops of the tincture to the affected area as needed for sunburn relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can soothe and heal the skin. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, lavender tincture has antimicrobial properties that can prevent infection and promote skin regeneration. When applied topically, it can help to hydrate and moisturize the skin, providing much-needed relief to sun-damaged skin. Overall, English lavender tincture is a natural and effective remedy for sunburn, offering a gentle and soothing solution to soothe and heal the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce sunburn-induced redness, swelling, and pain by inhibiting the release of inflammatory chemicals, thanks to its active constituents, such as linalyl acetate and linalool.
Analgesic: Its analgesic property aids in relieving sunburn-related pain and discomfort by desensitizing pain receptors, attributed to the presence of compounds like cineole and camphor.

Wound-healing: The tincture's wound-healing property promotes skin regeneration and tissue repair, helping sunburned skin recover more quickly, due to the presence of active constituents like tannins and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: English lavender tincture's antioxidant property helps protect skin cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by sunburn-induced free radicals, thanks to the presence of antioxidant compounds like rosmarinic acid and apigenin.
Moisturizing: Its moisturizing property helps soothe and hydrate sunburned skin, preventing dryness and flaking, due to the presence of emollient compounds like triglycerides and sterols.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, English lavender flowers help soothe sunburned skin and promote healing.
Leaves: Containing therapeutic oils, lavender leaves can alleviate pain and inflammation caused by sunburn when used in medicinal tea.
Buds: High in soothing properties, lavender buds can help reduce redness and irritation associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest English lavender flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry jar halfway.
Add 80-proof vodka until flowers are completely submerged, stirring gently to release air bubbles.
Seal jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking gently once a week.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any remaining liquid.
Label bottle with ingredients, preparation date, and recommended dosage (e.g., 1-2 droppersful on sunburned area, up to 3 times daily).

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with sunburn because it contains hypericin and hyperforin, two compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These components help reduce inflammation, redness, and swelling caused by sunburn. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture promotes skin repair by stimulating the production of new skin cells, which can help soothe sunburned skin and alleviate pain. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of St. John's wort tincture help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which are produced in excess during sun exposure. Overall, St. John's wort tincture can be an effective natural remedy for sunburn, providing relief and promoting skin healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain. This property is due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin.
Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing, helping sunburned skin recover faster. This is attributed to the flavonoids and essential oils found in St. John's wort.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation, reducing the risk of long-term sun damage. The antioxidant effect is primarily due to the flavonoids and hypericin.
Analgesic: The tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the discomfort associated with sunburn. This is mainly due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin.
Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in sunburned skin. This is attributed to the hypericin and essential oils in the tincture.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, the flowers of St. John's Wort are used to make medicinal tea for sunburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar compounds as the flowers, and they are used in tea preparation to help soothe sunburned skin and promote healing.
Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, stems of St. John's Wort still contain beneficial compounds and are occasionally included in medicinal tea blends for sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar about 3/4 full.
Submerge the plant material with 80-proof vodka, ensuring all parts are covered by at least 1-2 inches.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the plant material and storing the liquid in a dark glass bottle.
Apply the tincture to sunburned skin as needed, using a cotton ball or spray bottle, avoiding open wounds or broken skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with sunburn because it contains allantoin, a substance that promotes the growth of new skin cells and helps to heal wounds. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Comfrey tincture can help soothe and moisturize the skin, providing relief from the discomfort of sunburn. Furthermore, it can help to speed up the healing process, reducing the time it takes for the sunburn to fade.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Wound healing: Comfrey tincture's wound healing abilities, attributed to allantoin and tannins, promote skin cell regeneration and help repair sun-damaged skin.
Pain relief: Comfrey tincture's pain-relieving properties, owing to rosmarinic acid and symphytine, alleviate discomfort caused by sunburn.
Moisturizing: Comfrey tincture's moisturizing effect, due to mucilage content, helps soothe and hydrate sunburned skin, preventing further damage and promoting healing.
Antioxidant: Comfrey tincture's antioxidant properties, derived from rosmarinic acid and tannins, protect sunburned skin from free radicals, reducing the risk of long-term skin damage.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried comfrey root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the chopped comfrey, ensuring all pieces are covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and apply to sunburn as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the comfrey plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for sunburn due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and protect sunburned skin.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Roots: The roots of the comfrey plant contain allantoin, a compound known for its skin-healing properties, making it a useful addition to medicinal tea for sunburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties that can soothe and repair damaged skin. The herb's active compounds, including allantoin, aid in the regeneration of skin cells and reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn. Furthermore, its demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and preventing further irritation. Plantain tincture's ability to alleviate pain and promote skin healing makes it an ideal natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain, due to the presence of flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin.
Wound healing: Plantain tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for sunburn recovery, as it contains allantoin, a substance known to accelerate tissue regeneration and repair.
Antioxidant: Plantain tincture neutralizes free radicals generated by sun exposure, preventing further skin damage, thanks to its high levels of phenolic acids, tannins, and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture inhibits bacterial growth on sunburned skin, minimizing the risk of infection, attributed to its tannins and essential oils.

Moisturizing: Plantain tincture hydrates and moisturizes sunburned skin, facilitating faster recovery and reducing peeling, due to its mucilaginous content, which forms a protective layer on the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in phenolic compounds, they possess soothing and calming effects, aiding in the relief of sunburn irritation.
Seeds: High in mucilage content, seeds help to form a protective layer on the skin, promoting faster healing of sunburned areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves from a clean, pesticide-free source, choosing young, healthy leaves for optimal potency.
Rinse leaves thoroughly, then roughly chop and add to a clean jar, filling it three-quarters full, before adding enough 100-proof vodka to cover the leaves completely.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for a minimum of two weeks, shaking it daily to promote even extraction.
After two weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean jar or bottle, squeezing out the leaves to extract all liquid.
Label the tincture with the ingredients and preparation date, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year, and applying it topically as needed for sunburn relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime tincture helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The lime extract in the tincture has high levels of vitamin C and flavonoids, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, it promotes the production of collagen, a protein that aids in skin repair and regeneration. The herbal properties of common lime tincture can help moisturize and hydrate the skin, preventing further damage and promoting a faster healing process. Overall, common lime tincture is a natural and effective remedy for sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Common lime tincture helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain, thanks to its flavonoid content.
Antioxidant: The high vitamin C content in lime tincture combats free radicals generated by UV radiation, protecting skin cells and promoting healing.
Analgesic: Lime tincture contains compounds that help alleviate pain, making it an effective remedy for the discomfort caused by sunburn.
Astringent: Lime tincture's astringent properties help to tighten and protect sunburned skin, reducing redness and swelling.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lime tincture help prevent infection in sunburned skin, supporting the skin's natural healing process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe sunburned skin and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in soothing properties, helping to alleviate pain and promote skin healing.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils that have a cooling effect on sunburned skin and aid in skin regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh lime leaves and stems, and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of 100-proof vodka and 1 cup of distilled water over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container.
Apply the tincture to sunburned skin as needed, diluting it with water if necessary.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with sunburn because it contains properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. It has anti-inflammatory and astringent qualities that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by sunburn. Witch hazel also contains tannins, which can help to repair damaged skin and promote new skin cell growth. Its cooling effect can provide instant relief to sunburned skin, making it an ideal natural remedy for sunburn. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help to protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate pain, swelling, and redness associated with sunburn. This property is due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils.
Astringent: It helps to tighten and dry out the skin, which can help to soothe the burning sensation and promote healing. The astringent properties come from the tannins present in witch hazel.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture neutralizes free radicals, protecting skin cells from further damage caused by sun exposure. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids found in witch hazel.

Antimicrobial: It helps to prevent bacterial infections, which can complicate sunburn wounds. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, such as eugenol and hexenol.

Soothing: Witch hazel tincture has a cooling effect on the skin, providing instant relief from the discomfort of sunburn. This soothing property is primarily due to the tannins and volatile oils present in witch hazel.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains high levels of tannins, making it effective in soothing sunburned skin and reducing inflammation.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, witch hazel leaves help to relieve pain, promote healing, and prevent infection in sunburned areas.
Flowers: Witch hazel flowers are astringent and mildly analgesic, making them useful in reducing swelling, redness, and discomfort caused by sunburn.
Twigs/Branches: High in gallic acid, these parts of the witch hazel plant aid in skin repair, reducing itching, and alleviating pain from sunburn.
Leaf Buds: Packed with beneficial compounds, the leaf buds of witch hazel are known for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to soothe and heal sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried witch hazel bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean jar or bottle, discarding the spent bark.
Label the tincture with the ingredients and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a few drops to sunburned skin as needed for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Arnica stimulates the flow of blood and oxygen to the affected area, promoting healing and tissue regeneration. Its soothing properties can help alleviate discomfort and itchiness, making it an effective natural remedy for sunburn. Additionally, arnica has been shown to speed up the healing process, reducing the time it takes for sunburned skin to return to its normal state.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture reduces inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain, due to its active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and helenalin.
Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture alleviates sunburn-related pain by numbing the affected area, thanks to its content of volatile oils such as thymol and camphor.
Increased blood flow: Arnica flower tincture improves blood circulation, facilitating the healing process and reducing swelling, owing to its active compounds like flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: Arnica flower tincture neutralizes free radicals generated by sun exposure, preventing further skin damage, as it contains high levels of antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Arnica flower tincture inhibits bacterial growth, protecting the sunburned skin from infections, attributed to its active constituents like essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The arnica flower contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful for soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the arnica plant also contain these beneficial compounds, and can be used in tea to help reduce redness and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the arnica plant contain similar beneficial compounds, and can be used as a substitute for the flowers or leaves when making medicinal tea for sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh Arnica montana flowers, filling a clean, dry mason jar two-thirds full.
Add 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged, covering by at least one inch.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, gently shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers and retaining the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to a clean, amber glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark location for up to one year. Apply as needed to soothe sunburned skin.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sunburn with herbal tinctures is by using carefully selected remedies that soothe, heal and reduce inflammation. A popular choice is St. John's Wort tincture, known for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Another effective option is Calendula tincture, which promotes tissue regeneration and eases pain. Apply the chosen tincture, diluted in water, to the affected area with a gentle, circular motion, several times a day. Combining these treatments with aloe vera gel can further enhance the soothing effects and support the skin's natural healing process.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal tinctures include mild burns, rashes, and skin irritations. For instance, calendula tincture is known for its anti-inflammatory and healing properties, making it an ideal treatment for sunburn and other skin irritations. St. John's Wort tincture is another popular choice for treating sunburn, as it contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration. Additionally, lavender tincture is often used to soothe and heal sunburned skin due to its analgesic and antimicrobial properties. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal tinctures, especially for severe burns or skin conditions.
